policy: Only the verified facts in these rows may enter runnable contracts. Off-screen or anime-expanded action must not be promoted as manga canon.

scenes.outcome_claim: Zoro exposes the trap and defeats the hundred Baroque Works bounty hunters across chapters 107 and 108.
visual_variant_notes: anonymous agents remain tableaux rather than named fighters
scenes.integration_note: Use the existing Whisky Peak island point only as the local theatre anchor; do not infer town topology from atlas jitter.

scenes.outcome_claim: Miss All Sunday appears aboard the Going Merry, flips Sanji and Usopp with her power, offers an Eternal Pose, and departs after Luffy rejects it.
visual_variant_notes: Hana-Hana arms only in the authored power pose
scenes.integration_note: A single-character introduction tableau avoids fabricating a prolonged fight.

scenes.outcome_claim: As Smoker is about to catch Luffy in Nanohana, Ace intervenes so Luffy and the crew can escape.
visual_variant_notes: protective interruption, not an invented extended duel
scenes.integration_note: Scene label deliberately avoids 'Ace versus Smoker'.

scenes.outcome_claim: After leaving the Going Merry, Ace destroys five ships carrying Baroque Works Billions with a single Fire Fist attack.
visual_variant_notes: the targets are Baroque Works ships, not Marine ships
five ships are grouped into one reusable convoy tableau
existing Arabasta Ace atlas remains valid
scenes.integration_note: At chapter 159 this becomes the newest story state at the Nanohana theatre anchor and supersedes the chapter-158 intervention tableau.

scenes.outcome_claim: Luffy stays behind after Rain Dinners and confronts Crocodile, but his ordinary attacks pass through the sand body; Crocodile defeats him and casts him into quicksand.
visual_variant_notes: Crocodile retains a readable human face through every sand state
scenes.integration_note: This illustrated scene supersedes the faceless rigid arabasta-luffy-crocodile-sand-study visual. Rounds two and three remain separate contracts.

scenes.outcome_claim: Sanji and Bon Clay exchange kick-based techniques across Alubarna; after understanding the Clone-Clone fruit's weakness, Sanji defeats him in chapter 189.
visual_variant_notes: Clone-Clone feint is compact and does not add a persistent copied actor
Ballet Kenpo remains readable without anime-only extensions
scenes.integration_note: The later first Diable Jambe versus Jabra remains a separate Enies Lobby progression scene.

scenes.outcome_claim: Nami learns the first Clima-Tact's weather-ball combinations under pressure and defeats Miss Doublefinger with Tornado Tempo across chapters 190 through 193.
visual_variant_notes: Spike-Spike transformations retain Miss Doublefinger's readable face
scenes.integration_note: At the shared Alubarna theatre this scene follows Sanji/Bon Clay and is replaced by Zoro/Mr. One when chapter 194 opens.

scenes.outcome_claim: Zoro fights Daz Bones across Alubarna, learns to cut steel after sensing its breath, and defeats Mr. 1 with a decisive one-sword Lion Song attack in chapter 195.
visual_variant_notes: Mr. 1's steel transformations keep his facial features visible
one-sword decisive finish follows the pressure from three swords
scenes.integration_note: Use reusable steel-spark, slash, and stone-debris FX; chapter 194 correctly replaces the preceding Nami/Miss Doublefinger scene at the shared Alubarna theatre.

scenes.outcome_claim: Luffy returns with water, makes Crocodile's sand body tangible, and lands hits, but Crocodile drains him; falling water restores Luffy after Crocodile leaves for the royal tomb.
visual_variant_notes: Crocodile's wet solid body and face remain readable
scenes.integration_note: Use a separate palace theatre and actor variants; never reuse the round-one quicksand outcome or final-tomb victory.

scenes.outcome_claim: In the underground royal tomb, Luffy uses restrained blood contact to strike Crocodile's sand body, survives the poison-hook escalation, and defeats him with an upward Gum-Gum Storm in chapter 210.
visual_variant_notes: blood countermeasure conveyed as non-graphic matte battle smears
Crocodile remains facially readable through defeat
scenes.integration_note: This is the trilogy resolution and must replace round two at the Alubarna theatre only when chapter 203 opens.
